query_normalized: llmxive follow-up: extending "arcane: do role-playing language agents stay in character at the right" linguistics
rationale: The paper focuses on improving LLM agent performance in tool-use and reasoning tasks by leveraging negative training examples, which is a distinct construct from the user's question about "staying in character" (role consistency) in role-playing agents. While both involve LLM agents, the mechanism (optimizing for task success vs. maintaining persona consistency) and the specific research gap (data efficiency vs. linguistic/behavioral fidelity) do not overlap sufficiently to satisfy the inclusion
